entry.title: Re: CDS for Customer hierarchy
entry.content:
Hello,

Has this issue been resolved? We also need to read Customer Hierarchies but there is no released API (CDS or ODATA). The Group reporting API doens't meet our requirement.

Regards

entry.title: Q : How to notify to user when invoices are not fully cleared in F110 (Payment run)
entry.content:
Hello,
I have a question regarding an issue similar to the one described in the below-mentioned SAP Notes.
3318007 - Error VF 348 in F110 - SAP for Me
I referred to SAP Note 3318007 and made the suggested changes, but there were no visible effects after implementing them.

In F110, when the user execute the Payment Run, it shows a green status with the message "Payment run has been carried out." However, I found that not all invoices are actually cleared.

Is there a way to modify the message to display a warning or error in such cases, using BTE, BAdI, or Enhancement?

It is important for users to know that the target invoices have been fully cleared.
Please let me know if there is an effective way to solve this.

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Create user by API on BTP subaccount
entry.content:
Hello @abiskop,

Thanks for the quick answer! I'll check, but I was wondering if there's some way of doing it by API for example posting it from Integration Suite as I would like to automatize it in some way when it is a large list of users.

Thank you very much.

Best regards.

entry.title: Batch Inbound Integration - Use of element ChangeOrdinalNumberValue
entry.content:
Hello,

We want to use SOAP API Batch Inbound Integration for connecting a third party WMS:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/9ce855c709ce4f5c99b685903461106e/d51b13a65cc744cf97a28a19e617d9dd.html?version=2408.500
https://api.sap.com/api/sap-s4-CE_BATCHBULKRPLCTNINFORMATION-v1/overview

for creating new batches and updating existing batches.
Not clear to us is the use of element ChangeOrdinalNumberValue. We noticed that setting a value for this field is required since SAP otherwise rejects/ignores this request (see screenshot).
Therefore we set ChangeOrdinalNumberValue to 1, as result the batch were created. For updating this batch we would need to set a greater ChangeOrdinalNumberValue.
Our first idea was to read the current ChangeOrdinalNumberValue via Batch Master Record ODATA API (similar to the eTag handling concept) but this API doesn't provide the ChangeOrdinalNumberValue in its response.

https://api.sap.com/api/API_BATCH_SRV/overview


Our current idea is to convert the current date/ date of the request to a unix timestamp and use this as ChangeOrdinalNumberValue so that newer requests have always a greater ChangeOrdinalNumberValue.

Questions is what is best practices of this ChangeOrdinalNumberValue handling when connecting a third party WMS? We don't want to force the WMS to store all ChangeOrdinalNumberValue in its system and looking for a automated solution.

We definitely want to use the SOAP API to have monitoring in AIF.

Thanks and best regards

Robert

entry.title: Re: Retraining Assignments / Self registration
entry.content:
We created a curriculum for a specific item that requires retraining every two years.

The required date varies depending on the training history.If an employee completed the training in 2023, they must retake it in 2025. If the training was completed in 2024, they will need to retake it in 2026. If an employee has never taken the training before, they do not receive any assignment. 

We scheduled five sessions in 2025 that are open for self registration. We would like to restrict access to these sessions only to those employees who need to be trained in 2025. Unfortunately, all employees who have been assigned the training currently have access to it.

entry.title: Re: FLP failed to load catalogs after system copy - what...
entry.content:
@hnitsch wrote:

Hello,

after systemcopy from testsystem to sandbox it's not possible to use Fiori. 

It is possible to log in to the FLP from sandbox, but then the message appears "Failed to load catalogs".

In testsystem FLP works fine.

What adjustments are necessary after a system copy? Who has experience with this?

thanks in advance

best regards Holger

Hello @hnitsch,
It sounds like you're experiencing an issue with the Fiori Launchpad (FLP) after performing a system copy from testsystem to sandbox. This is a known issue, and there are a few adjustments you might need to make: Your Texas Benefitsi

  1. Check System Aliases: Ensure that the system aliases are correctly pointing to the new sandbox system.

  2. SSO Configuration: Verify that Single Sign-On (SSO) is properly configured with the backend system.

  3. Web Dispatcher: Make sure that the new sandbox server is correctly added to the Web Dispatcher for routing requests.

  4. Post-Installation Steps: Follow the necessary post-installation steps, such as consistency checks and removing unnecessary RFC connections.

If you've already tried these steps and the issue persists, it might be helpful to reach out to SAP Support for further assistance.

Best regards,
Peter Philip

entry.title: Re: Usage and differences between MRS , RSH and FSM
entry.content:
Hi Radhakmk,

It is possible to continue using SAP MRS (SAP MRS FOR S4HANA 1.0) with an end of mainstream maintenance unitl 31.12.2030. This is applicable for versions of SAP S/4HANA until SAP S/4HANA 2023. For SAP ECC the EoMM is 31.12.2027, with the option of extended maintenance until 31.12.2030 (this would have additional costs/contract, etc.). For a conversion from SAP ECC to SAP S/4HANA, this would be possible but best to discuss with your dedicated account team on what makes most sense for your company. Generally we would advise customers to start planning to migrate from SAP MRS to SAP FSM, or SAP RSH, more info in this blog. 

Kind regards,
Ryan

entry.title: Re: SAP Universal ID S users - issue
entry.content: Thanks a lot. I sent an email to sso@sap.com and it got sorted.

entry.title: How to notify to user when invoices are not fully cleared in F110 (Payment run)
entry.content:
Hello,
I have a question regarding an issue similar to the one described in the below SAP Notes.
3318007 - Error VF 348 in F110 | SAP Knowledge Base Article
I referred to SAP Note 3318007 and made the suggested changes, but there were no visible effects after implementing them.


In F110, when the user execute the Payment Run, it shows a green light with the message "Payment run has been carried out." However, I found that not all invoices are actually cleared.

Is there a way to modify the message to display a warning or error in such cases, using BTE, BAdI, or Enhancement?

It is important for users to be informed whether invoices have been fully cleared or partially cleared.
Please let me know if there is an effective way to solve this.

Thank you.

entry.title: Digital Signature configuration for Flexible number range for billing document.
entry.content:
Hi Experts

We are trying flexible number range for one our entity were our number range prefix have alphanumeric combination. And we want to use same number range interval in the following configuration (Record Number Ranges for Digital Signature of Billing Documents (SD). But we couldn't able to add those intervals. Any help how we can configure this for flexible number range. Thanks in Advance.

Regards,

Raj Kumar G

entry.title: UI5 - Failed to load 'sap/uxap/ObjectPageLayout.js'
entry.content:
Hello, I'm currently doing a UI5 course and I've encountered a problem with sap.uxap library in this chapter . I used code provided in the course and I'm trying to use sap.uxap elements in Detail.view.xml

 

<mvc:View controllerName="student02.com.sap.training.ux402.listdetail.ux402listdetail.controller.Detail"
    xmlns:mvc="sap.ui.core.mvc"
    xmlns="sap.m"
    xmlns:ux="sap.uxap"
    xmlns:layout="sap.ui.layout">
    <ux:ObjectPageLayout id="objectPageLayout">
        <ux:headerTitle>
            <ux:ObjectPageDynamicHeaderTitle id="objectPageDynamicHeader">
                <ux:expandedHeading>
                    <Title
                        id="title1"
                        text="{Carrname}"
                        level="H2"/>
                </ux:expandedHeading>
            </ux:ObjectPageDynamicHeaderTitle>
        </ux:headerTitle>
        <ux:headerContent>
            <FlexBox wrap="Wrap" id="flexBox">
                <Avatar id="avatar" src="sap-icon://flight"/>
                <layout:VerticalLayout id="verticalLayout1" class="sapUiSmallMarginBeginEnd">
                    <Label id="label1" text="{Carrid}"/>
                    <Label id="label2" text="{Url}"/>
                </layout:VerticalLayout>
            </FlexBox>
        </ux:headerContent>
        <ux:sections>
            <ux:ObjectPageSection id="objectPageSection">
                <ux:ObjectPageSubSection id="objectPageSubSection1">
                    <Table id="table" headerText="{i18n>tableHeaderText}" items="{to_Connection}" 
                           noDataText="{i18n>tableNoDataText}">
                        <columns>
                            <Column id="column1">
                                <Text id="text1" text="{i18n>idColumnText}"/>
                            </Column>
                            <Column id="column2">
                                <Text id="text2" text="{i18n>cityFromColumnText}"/>
                            </Column>
                            <Column id="column3">
                                <Text id="text3" text="{i18n>cityToColumnText}"/>
                            </Column>
                        </columns>
                        <items>
                            <ColumnListItem id="columnListItem">
                                <cells>
                                    <Text id="text4" text="{Carrid}"/>
                                    <Text id="text5" text="{Cityfrom}"/>
                                    <Text id="text6" text="{Cityto}"/>
                                </cells>
                            </ColumnListItem>
                        </items>
                    </Table>
                </ux:ObjectPageSubSection>
            </ux:ObjectPageSection>
        </ux:sections>
    </ux:ObjectPageLayout>
</mvc:View>

 

Then, when I click on the button that routes to this view I get an error in the browser 

I also tried to access resources link from which ObjectPageLayout.js should be accessed and there is nothing in the uxap repository ( https://port8080-workspaces-ws-clgqm.us10.trial.applicationstudio.cloud.sap/resources/sap )

Does this mean that the error is on the side of API and not in my project?

Thanks in advance for responses.

Michal

entry.title: Re: Best Practices for Utilizing Concur’s Identity v4 and User Provisioning v4 Webservices
entry.content:
Hi @RickF,

thanks for the blog series.

I have a question related to SAP IPS and role assignment in SAP Concur.
When I have understand it correct, the SAP IPS is using the Concur Identity V4 API to provisioning users. In the Identity V4 API documentation is nothing mentioned related to roles.

I tried the path from the UPS API (urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:spend:2.0:Role) but it is saying, that it doesnt know this targetpath.

Do you have any ideas?

The user provisioning itself works already, but not the role assignment (EXP_USER, REQ_USER

Thanks,
Vincent

entry.title: Re: Configuring the proxy CO_CRM_ISX_CC_CATALOG_SERVICES
entry.content:
Hi Eric,

thank you for your comment. SAP Basis version is 750 and according to the note, re-creating the Consumer Proxy using the same WSDL as the one used for the Logical Port in SOAMANAGER was proposed.



I did what was suggested but still get the error: 

The similar behavior was observed also in another CRM system on another client. Could it be that the v3 version of the Catalog WS is not compatible with CO_CRM_ISX_CC_CATALOG_SERVICES? In S4Hana system the proxy CO_CRMS4_SOM_CC_V3_CATALOG_SER can be very easily configured with the V3 WS. 

Thank you,
Doris

entry.title: Copy Custom field from service order to PR
entry.content:
Hi

I want to define a custom field at solution order header/item level and want it to be copied to service order and eventually on the PR that gets created from the service order. 

I don't see a business scenario where I can enable such copying. There are options to enable copying from service transaction to sales transactions. But nothing to copy to a purchase requisition. 

Any help is appreciated. 

Regards,

Deepti

entry.title: SAP CTS/TMS: disable step "Report and screen generation" during a transport import
entry.content:
Hello NetWeaver ABAP basis community,
during a CTS transport import (done by STMS_IMPORT), there is the step "Report and screen generation" (example of steps during import is here) which starts to compile the imported objects.

We like to disable this step per transport, per system whatever is possible. Does anybody know if/how this could be possible ?

Thanks & best regards,
 Matthias

I darkly remember a option in SAINT/SPAM where it was possible to set somewhat like "always", "as SAP requests", "never" but this seems not available in STMS for "regular" (no SPAM/SAINT) transports.

entry.title: Re: SAP Community - Where are you from?
entry.content: My area of expertise lies in SAP consulting, with experience specializing in the FI and SD modules. I have extensive experience in customizations, internal developments, and integrations with third-party systems. My work involves designing and implementing tailored solutions to meet business needs, ensuring seamless process optimization and compliance with financial regulations.

entry.title: Re: Copy Custom field from service order to PR
entry.content:
Hello,

Define the Custom Field in Solution Order
You can create the custom field at the solution order level (either at the header or item level) in your system using the relevant object or master data extension (e.g., custom field extension in SAP C4C, Salesforce, or other ERP/CRM systems).
Ensure the field is available on both the solution order header and item levels.
2. Transfer the Custom Field to the Service Order
In systems like SAP, Salesforce, or others with order management capabilities, you typically use a function module, BADI (Business Add-In), or a similar mechanism to populate the custom field from the solution order to the service order.
If your system allows copying fields between different order types (like solution orders to service orders), you might need to create a custom enhancement for this purpose. This could be a trigger, user exit, or workflow action that copies the custom field from the solution order to the service order when certain conditions are met.
3. Linking the Service Order to Purchase Requisition
Since there is no standard functionality to copy directly to a Purchase Requisition (PR) from a service order, you will need to implement custom logic for this step.
If your service order is integrated with your procurement system (e.g., SAP, Oracle), you can set up a custom process that automatically transfers data from the service order (including the custom field) to the PR. This might involve writing a custom program or BADI to fetch the service order data, including the custom field, and then populate the corresponding field in the PR when it's created.
4. Custom Field Mapping for PR Creation
Create a custom mapping between the service order and the PR. This might involve extending the PR creation logic so that the custom field from the service order is passed onto the PR during the PR generation process.
Depending on your system, this can either be done through a manual step (e.g., using workflows or approval processes) or by automating it through system scripts or custom integrations.
5. Testing and Validation
Ensure you test the entire flow (solution order → service order → purchase requisition) to verify that the custom field is correctly populated at each stage.
It's also a good practice to check that the field remains intact and updated if the service order undergoes any changes after the PR is created.

 

Best Regards
MyFordBenefits website

entry.title: Re: Start authorization for R3TR IWSV API_PURCHASEREQ_PROCESS_SRV 0001, return code 4
entry.content: Did you solve it?

entry.title: Re: "/DMO/AGENCY_D" is not a suitable draft persistency f...
entry.content: This issue still happens 20/01/2025. I tried to create a case in SAP Learning Self Service, and they just said they don't support learning journey issues.

entry.title: Re: How to analyse BEx Query performance using "RSRT" on...
entry.content:
Hi,
the core functions in RSRT remain same in BW/4HANA, there are some enhancements, but that should be all.
Press "Execute and Debug", select "collect Statistics" + "Co not use Cache" and run the query.
When you see the query result, use the green arrow to go back.
Finally you should see the runtimes. The event IDs starting 9* are the DB times, the rest is mainly OLAP processing time.
Regards - Frank

Miguel

entry.content:
Hi @RobertWaywell / @Dan_vL , 

Thanks for taking your time in checking my query.

This is to confirm that upgrading HANA Cloud DB to version 2024.40.10 (QRC 4/2024) will fix this issue. We tried from our end. It worked!.

Good Day

However, it fails because the DMIS_CNT2011_1_731.SAR archive doesn’t exist, its not being downloaded via the maintenance planner probably due to license or authorization issues.

entry.title: Enhancing the eDocument Cockpit with BAdI EDOC_CUSTOM_FIELDS_CLOUD
entry.content:
In this blog post we’ll describe how you can use the BAdI method FILL_EDOC_CUSTOM_FIELDS of BAdI EDOC_CUSTOM_FIELDS_CLOUD to extend the eDocument Cockpit (EDOC_COCKPIT) with custom fields. This enhancement allows you to add your own custom fields to display data from various source documents like billing, accounting and delivery.

Please note that these customizations apply only to new electronic documents generated after the implementation.

Prerequisites

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Developer Extensibility

Steps to be implemented

  • Creation of the custom field
  • Implementation of the custom logic

Creation of the custom field

  1. Go to Custom Fields app.
  2. Select Create to add a new custom field. In this example, we’ll add the “Total Weight” field.

3. Define the labels of the custom field and choose Publish.

The publishing process takes some time. Once it's published, you can move on to the implementation steps in ADT (ABAP Development Tools).

Implementation Steps in ABAP Development Tools (ADT)

  1. In your ABAP project, go to the enhancement spot ES_DOCUMENT_CLOUD (path: package GLO-EDO > Enhancements > Enhancement Spots).
  2. Right-click ES_EDOCUMENT_CLOUD and select New BAdI Enhancement Implementation.
  3. Enter a package, a name, and a description.
  4. Choose Add BAdI Implementation and select EDOC_CUSTOM_FIELDS_CLOUD.
  5. Create a BAdI filter for process.
  6. Choose Implementing class to create a new implementation class. The new implementation class automatically implements the interfaces "if_badi_interfaces" and "if_edoc_custom_fields_cloud".
  7. After generating the class, open the interface method and press F3 to view its parameters. To display the documentation, press F2.
  8. Activate your BAdI implementation.

Example Implementation

In the following example, we’ll fill the Total weight and the Unit of weight of the delivery to the custom field “yy1_totalweight_edo” and “yy1_totalweight_edou”  which was created in the previous step.

Coding:

 

 

CLASS zcl_edoc_custom_fields_cloud_t DEFINITION
  PUBLIC
  FINAL
  CREATE PUBLIC .

  PUBLIC SECTION.

    INTERFACES if_badi_interface .
    INTERFACES if_edoc_custom_fields_cloud .
  PROTECTED SECTION.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
ENDCLASS.

CLASS zcl_edoc_custom_fields_cloud_t IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD if_edoc_custom_fields_cloud~fill_edoc_custom_fields.

*For Process Mexico Delivery Note - Filling btgew and gewei ( Total Weight and Unit of Weight )
    IF process = 'MXDELN'.
        IF source_data-delivery_header-btgew IS NOT INITIAL AND source_data-delivery_header-gewei IS NOT INITIAL.
          custom_fields-yy1_totalweight_edo = source_data-delivery_header-btgew.
          custom_fields-yy1_totalweight_edou = source_data-delivery_header-gewei.
        ENDIF.
    ENDIF.

  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

 

 

Result:

When you display a newly created Electronic document in the eDocument Cockpit, you’ll see that the Total weight and the Unit of weight of the delivery is filled.

Give us your feedback
To ensure that we’re covering what you need, please leave your feedback at the bottom of this blog post!
#SAPGoGlobal #SAPLocalization

entry.title: Re: What is the difference between API Provider, API Prox...
entry.content:
APIProvider is an company or entity that create and offer API and in essentially provides data ,functionality to other application.


Were as API Proxy act as a intermediator b/w Client application and Actual API and providing additional functionality such as Security ,rate limit, monitoring without changing the actual backend API. essentially it is a controlled Access point to the API with added functionality.

entry.title: Custom Movement Types in Inventory Management Fiori apps - Part 2
entry.content:
Introduction 

Custom movement types offer you the flexibility to individualize the movement of goods in your system to meet your specific business needs.  

Fiori apps that support custom movement types include: Goods Receipt for Production Order, Goods Receipt for Purchasing Document, Goods Receipt for Inbound Delivery, Goods Receipt for Process Order, and Post Subsequent Adjustment. 

The implementation of custom movement types in your system is a 2-step process: 

  1. Use the configuration activity Copy of Movement Type (ID 103898) to Copy an Existing Movement Type. For more information on how to do this see the following blog post: Custom Movement Types in Inventory Management Fiori apps 
  2. Implement the BAdI Change Header and Item Data in Goods Receipts MMIM_GR4XY_PROVIDE_DATA.   

This blog will focus on step 2: the implementation of the BAdI Change Header and Item Data in Goods Receipts.

Prerequisites:  

  • An existing custom movement type. 
  • For the Custom Logic app: An Administrator or Extensibility Specialist role is required. 
  • For the Goods Receipt for Purchasing Document app: An Inventory Manager or Warehouse Clerk role is required. 

Example Implementation  

Let's say that you need to use movement type 101 -post goods receipt - but want to post the goods receipt to a different account. You have already done the first step and copied an existing movement type to create a new movement type Z01. (See the example here: Custom Movement Types in Inventory Management Fiori apps.) 

After creating your custom movement type, you must implement the BAdI MMIM_GR4XY_PROVIDE_DATA to enable the use of your new movement type in one of the supported Goods Receipt Fiori apps. 

Here is how you can do this: 

Start by opening the Custom Logic app and clicking on Create in the table toolbar. 

From the Business Context drop-down, choose Inventory Management: Goods Receipt for Fiori apps PurOrd, ProdnOrd, NoRef, InbDeliv. 

Choose the Extension Point: Change Header and Item Data in Goods Receipts (MMIM_GR4XY_PROVIDE DATA) and choose Step 2.  

Enter an Implementation Description of your choosing. For this example, I will use CUSTOM_MVMT_TEST. The Implementation ID field will be filled automatically: YY1_ CUSTOM_MVMT_TEST. Choose Review 

Review the details. If everything looks good, choose Create.  

To add your specific code, choose Publish.

You can now click the Open Code Editor.  

In the code editing screen, you can now see a space to edit your code and another space for the parameters that are available for the BAdI. In the space for editing, you can choose to edit your implementation directly or, in this case, copy the sample code. To do this, you click on Show Sample Code: 

A pop-up appears. Choose Copy to Clipboard.  

After you copy the sample code, choose the Edit button. 

Paste the sample code into the editor. You will have to remove the first comment paragraphs and uncomment the rest of the code. (See below.) This example shows how you would enable your custom movement Z01 for use in the Goods Receipt for Purchasing Document Fiori app. 

 

Choose Save and Publish. 

Now that you have created this implementation, you can see your custom movement type in action. After you post a goods receipt for a purchasing document, you will see your custom movement type listed in the material document. 

As an added bonus, you can use the BAdI MMIM_GR4XY_CHECK_DATA to implement your own validation logic for your custom movement type.  

For more details, see Custom Logic and App Extensibility: Post Goods Receipt for Purchasing Document. 

I hope this blog helps you to create your own custom movement type implementation! If you found this information helpful and want to see more like it, please give this article a thumbs up!

entry.title: Re: Error occured while settling the Asset AUC
entry.content: Hi, Thanks for your query, before you post the question kindly check the SAP community forum which may helpful and had the resolution https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/check-amount-entered-amount-is-too-high-message-no-aa317/qaq-p/397116

entry.title: Re: Error occured while settling the Asset AUC

Hi, 

Thanks for your question, i would suggest to go through with existing queries posted related to this subject.

Solved: Check amount entered (Amount is too high) Message ... - SAP Community

 

Regards

Mansoor Pathan

entry.title: GST Reconciliation Report - India
entry.content:
Hello all,

We've a requirement that business wants to have a proper tax report which includes all the data related to GST as well as TDS apart from DRC Reporting (GSTR1, GSTR3B, GSTR6, FORM26Q, FORM27Q and FORM27EQ). 

Is there any application in SAP Public Cloud which will provide the data for all GST and TDS transactions? For other countries we've used "Tax Declaration Reconciliation" app but this will not work for India Localization

Please help me out as on this topic as soon as possible.

Thank you

entry.title: Re: Failed to get access token, contact your administrator
entry.content:
Hi,

Check if this KBA 3297866 - Error - 'Failed to get access token, contact your administrator' is helpful for your issue.

Kr,

Jitin

entry.title: Re: creating a structure, but data element not active
entry.content:
I think your are using the field name, not the data element name( like werks and werks_d)
please check.

Br,
Marwen

entry.title: Re: Simplification List for Plant Maintenance (Enterprise Asset Management)
entry.content:
This is a really useful resouce, thanks for sharing this!

I want to expand on item 6 S4TWL - Changes In List Reports For Order and Notification as I think the SAP documentation in this note could use some more detailed information.

The transparent table PMFLAGS is updated when running reports "QM_PHASE_FILL" and "RIAFVC_IPHAS_FILL" with FLAG03 and FLAG01 respectively. Checking this table is the easiest way to conifrm if the reports have been run.

This might be obvious to other users but I would've been helped by the following information:

  • An exception is brought up in Note 3534140 which explains that for AUFK-AUTYP=10,40 the fields AUFK_PHAS0,1,2,3 are not relevant. As I understand it, when running RIAFVC_IPHAS_FILL phases will not be implemented for Order Category 10 (Production Order) and 40 (Process Order) as these use a different concept of phases.

can you please explain me the customizing to extract all my data for DAS2.

 

thks in advance.

BR

Thank you,
Ganjos

Noel

entry.title: Re: SAP IBP - Q : Can we copy current week data from Sour...
entry.content:
Hello, 

Without knowing the full context, I would like to propose a solution.

If you go to the Copy Operator profile > Selection > Time section, you will see a Period Shift (Copy Level) parameter. This parameter shifts the copy bucket to another. 

Combining this with the application job template approach, you can define three steps of copy that copies a key figure from the previous period.

I hope this helps.

entry.title: Re: SAP Datsphere TRIAL is not functional anymore
entry.content: Thank you. Redoing the role has worked.

entry.title: Apply both HSN Code & SAC Code on same material Code
entry.content:

We are using the same material codes for both manufacturing as well as Job work purpose. Currently we have maintained HSN Codes on all the materials. However, our client requires that when we are manufacturing the material the HSN code should be picked (as happening currently) but in job work scenario the SAC code should be picked. Kindly suggest how we can maintain both HSN & SAC code for same material code.

 

Regards.

entry.title: Re: Key figure for when expected value is 0 - analysis wo...
entry.content: Hi Clóvis. Thanks for your comment. This is the solution to filter indeed, however you can not create a key figure based on this, which is required for subsequent calculations. Having spoken to SAP I believe the only option is SDK. Thanks for taking the time to comment! 🙂

entry.title: SAP B1 Bin Location Storage suggestion algorithm
entry.content:
Hello,

Based on a requirement from a customer, we have bin locations, each with a specific format type.

Let’s take an example with the format: PAL.

We would like the system to calculate the volume for each item with the PAL format. The calculation would be based on the formula:
UVF = (Height x Width x Length) x Quantity

The system should then suggest the best bin location with the PAL format that is not already full.

For example:

  • Bin Location A-01, format: PAL, is currently at 90% of its maximum Unit of Volume Format (UVF).
  • Bin Location A-02, format: PAL, is at 20% of its UVF.

If we have an item with a volume of 20 UVF, the system would propose storing it in A-02, as it has more available capacity.

Regarding the system's ability to make such proposals, this functionality would require development. However, I would like to know how this could be achieved using SBO transactions or procedures.

Thanks,

entry.title: Re: Taking Groups to the Next Level with Application-Specific Groups
entry.content:
Hello,

I have setup the above in a trial account, first getting the role collections from a BTP Subaccount setup as source system to the SAP CIS Local Identity Directory as Target System. This works fine... But after adding a second BTP subaccount to make it a bit more "real life" I run into some problems.

The provisioning job of the second BTP subaccount target system overwrites the Groups / Role Collections that are present in both subaccounts. It uses the displayName to search for the group and updates it. What is best approach to force creating new groups when the applicationId is different?

Also when provisioning two BTP subaccounts (target system) from the Local Identity Directory (Source System) it tries to provision all available groups. What is the best approach to set a filter to provision only the roles related to the applicationId? For example similar to setting xsuaa.group.prefix at the target system level…

Any pointers in the right direction are much appreciated.... If I am overlooking the obvious, my appologies....

entry.title: SAP Sales Cloud V2 (Partner Update)
entry.content:
During this session, our experts will provide updates on the following:

  1. Sales (and Service) Momentum - A look at the successes and huge opportunities
  2. GROW with SAP Sales Cloud - A look at how integrating SAP Sales Cloud to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition improves sales processes with SAP Business AI, reduces TCO and increases bottom line growth
  3. Field Sales Add-on - A look at the latest addition to the SAP Sales Cloud Solution area
  4. Joule in SAP Sales Cloud - Showcasing how SAP's AI Assistant and other embedded AI tools can connect sales processes, remove complexity and increase efficiency and performance Sales and Service
  5. MOVE Strategy - A look at the tools, guidance, support and services available to help Partners transition their customers to Sales and Service V2
  6. Q&A

Register Here

entry.title: Multiple Asset Recording through MIRO
entry.content:
Hello,

I have received a purchase order (PO) with two materials (fixed assets) that were identified as dummy assets. Through the FI module, we have created 200 assets and intend to book them.

However, when we reference the respective PO, two materials appear as dummy assets in MIRO.

When I try to allocate these two to the already created fixed assets, the system only allows six of them.

My question is: how can I book these 200 assets in MIRO? Can anyone help me with this?

Thank you in advance,

entry.title: Re: Transfer of documents from content server 1 to content server 2
entry.content: Migrate ArchiveLink Documents from 1 Content Repository to another

entry.title: Moving SAP S/4HANA Change Management to the 21st Century using DevOps Methodologies
entry.content:
Moving SAP S/4HANA Change Management to the 21st Century with DevOps Methodologies and SAP Standard Tools

It is surprising to see that a relatively low number of clients are leveraging the full potential of the standard functionality Git-enabled Change and Transport System (CTS) in the latest versions of SAP S/4HANA.

Many organizations are still managing SAP S/4HANA changes with the reminiscent basic SAP transport functionality available since SAP R/3 3.0F at the 90’s.

Traditional approaches, while familiar, often fall short in addressing the dynamic needs of modern enterprises, just to mentioned some like:

The lack of integration with contemporary DevOps practices can lead to inefficiencies, slower delivery cycles, and increased risk of errors.

The Power of Git-Enabled CTS

  1. SAP's Git-enabled CTS bridges the gap between legacy systems and modern DevOps methodologies. By integrating Git with SAP S/4HANA, organizations can achieve:
  2. Improved Version Control: Git provides robust version control capabilities, enabling teams to track changes, manage versions, and collaborate more effectively.
  3. Better Collaboration: Distributed version control allows multiple developers to work on the same project simultaneously, reducing bottlenecks and improving productivity.
  4. Enhanced Automation: Integrating Git with CI/CD pipelines automates the deployment process, ensuring faster, more reliable releases.
  5. Streamlined Processes: DevOps practices, supported by Git-enabled CTS, facilitate continuous integration and continuous delivery (CI/CD), leading to more agile and responsive change management.

Why the Slow Adoption?

The slow adoption of Git-enabled CTS in SAP S/4HANA environments can be attributed to several factors:

  • Lack of Awareness: Many organizations are not fully aware of the capabilities and benefits of Git-enabled CTS.
  • Resistance to Change: Moving away from established practices can be challenging, especially for teams accustomed to traditional tools.
  • Skill Gaps: There may be a lack of expertise in both DevOps methodologies and the specific integration of Git with SAP S/4HANA.

Moving Forward

To move SAP S/4HANA change management from the late 90s to the 21st century, organizations need to:

  1.             Educate: Raise awareness about the benefits of Git-enabled CTS and provide training to bridge skill gaps.
  2.             Adopt DevOps Practices: Embrace DevOps methodologies to foster a culture of continuous improvement and collaboration.
  •             Leverage SAP Standard Tools: Utilize the full potential of SAP's standard tools, such as Git-enabled CTS, to streamline and modernize change management processes.

Conclusion

The standard functionality Git-enabled CTS in SAP S/4HANA is a powerful tool that can transform change management practices, aligning them with modern DevOps methodologies. By overcoming the barriers to adoption, organizations can enhance their agility, efficiency, and overall performance. It's time to move SAP S/4HANA change management into the 21st century.

entry.title: Re: Batch Inbound Integration - Use of element "ChangeOrd...
entry.content:
Hello Robert,

thanks for your question.

In the SOAP infrastructure, the system ensures that each message will be processed exactly once. However, it does not ensure that the processing always takes place in the order in which the changes of a transferred entity have taken place. Therefore, an ever increasing value is needed in the message payload to check in the inbound processing whether a message for a given batch and sender (the so-called sequencing context) can processed or must be ignored. This value is the change ordinal number value.

The sender of SOAP messages is responsible for ensuring this constraint. One option is that the sender stores and updates this value locally and handles it as counter. Note that one counter is then needed per combination Batch + recipient. By the way, this is also the approach chosen by the SOAP outbound integration.

If you aim at averting that local storage and counter handling, you could convert timestamps to integers and use that as an ordinal value - note that this is allowed since the change ordinal number values may contain gaps. The only downside is the lack of transparency on the recipient side - you'll always see gaps in the monitoring tools without being able to deduce whether some messages are missing.

Kind Regards,

Philipp

entry.title: Re: Pulling Job short text in Ad Hoc query

entry.content:
Hi Community,

We are planning to use the Auto-Translate feature in our Ariba SAP Companion. The feature is great, but we would like to exclude our English business terms from the translation. For example, we would like to retain the English term "Purchase Requisition".

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Re: SAP Customer checkout Retail Service UI Issue - Payme...
entry.content:
Hi All,

The UI you can update from CCO manager from the POS configuration - Quick Selection and you can link with user master UI.

Thank you...

entry.title: Re: SAP B1 Bin Location Storage suggestion algorithm
entry.content:
Hello,

As you cannnot make update or insert directly in the database the best approach is to block via transaction notification the allocation in the wrong bin location. Here is the query to get the position assigned to a line in Sales Order (ORDR). You can use as an example to build your logic and replicate for the others marketing documents.

SELECT OBTL."BinAbs" AS "BinAbsEntry", OBTL."Quantity", OITL."DocLine" 
FROM OITL
INNER JOIN OBTL ON OBTL."ITLEntry" = OITL."LogEntry"
WHERE OITL."DocType" = 17
AND OITL."DocEntry" = list_of_cols_val_tab_del

 

Hope it helps.

Rajib Hossain

entry.content:
Hello,
Please check the SAP Help Portal for more information on how the editability of key figures works.

For a static approach you can use configuration but for more dynamic one you can use editability horizon.

Cascading of the Editability of Key Figures | SAP Help Portal
Best regards

entry.title: Re: How to fix datasphere DATE format for CSV file going to LOCAL TABLE
entry.content: I do not know the full context, but it is quite common to define date format as a string, and then do convertion of dates into the date format in the next step, if possible. Regards.

entry.title: Re: How can we freeze the future bucket (Non editable) in...
entry.content: As per requirement, we need to freeze the cureent and future periods (For Example Jan-2025 and Feb-2025 both the periods need to lock). as per standard setting we can freeze current bucket but how can we lock future (Feb-2025) buckets in existing key figure?

entry.title: Re: 🟢 Extending your On-Premise OData Entities in CAP
entry.content:
Dear Jorg and Katan,

Thank you for the insightful demo and excellent example. It was highly relevant to the real-world challenges we frequently encounter. Your explanation was very clear and helpful.

I have a specific scenario where I need to implement bidirectional associations between two entities within the same remote service. Both entities are not persisted locally, and I am facing difficulties in achieving all the features that come with associations, such as $expand and others. The operations I need to implement include:

  • .../service/entity1('...')/entity2

  • .../service/entity1?$expand=entity2&$filter=...

  • .../service/entity1?$select=...

Could you provide an example or guidance on how to handle navigation between two remote entities within the same or different remote services, without extending the remote service on the remote server?

Your assistance in this matter would be greatly appreciated.

Best regards, Rakesh

entry.title: Re: SAP CTS/TMS: disable step "Report and screen generati...
entry.content:
I'm not sure but you may try the transport profile parameter BLOCK_LOAD_GENERATION (file DIR_TRANS/bin/TP_<domain>.PFL). Its possible values are LOCKED and DELAYED. It affects the whole system. It's described in the SAP note 1899663 - ABAP loads with errors following import during routine operations.

I understand this parameter is used in case the kernel detects that the ABAP Load needs to be regenerated, but it's not clear whether it's used when the concerned program is executed/loaded or when the generation is forced (especially in the case the program is transported).
